title: 2024 PHB Rules Overview
summary: High-level summary of all major changes between the 2014 and 2024 Player's Handbook editions.
content:
## 2024 PHB Rules Overview

*Comparison: 5e 2014 vs 5e 2024*

The 2024 Player's Handbook is a revised and updated version of the 2014 PHB. It is **backward compatible** with existing 5e adventures and supplements, but introduces significant mechanical changes across every pillar of the game.

### Scope of Changes

The 2024 PHB revises:
- **All 12 core classes** with updated features, subclass structures, and progression tables
- **Character creation** process (new step order, backgrounds grant ASIs)
- **Combat rules** (weapon mastery, surprise, opportunity attacks, two-weapon fighting)
- **Spellcasting** (multi-target damage, ritual casting, cantrip retraining)
- **Feats** (origin feats at level 1, fighting styles as feats, epic boons at 19)
- **Conditions** (Bloodied reintroduced, revised Hide rules)
- **Terminology** (Race→Species, new d20 Test umbrella term)
- **Resting** (long rest cooldown, full hit dice recovery)

### Design Philosophy

The 2024 revision focuses on:
1. **Player agency** — more choices at every level, fewer dead levels
2. **Clarity** — standardized language, reduced ambiguity
3. **Balance** — weaker classes buffed (Monk, Ranger, Sorcerer), dominant strategies reined in (Paladin crit smite removed)
4. **Flexibility** — species decoupled from ability scores, backgrounds are customizable

### Compatibility

- 2024 classes work with 2014 subclasses (with minor DM adjudication)
- 2014 adventures run without modification
- 2014 and 2024 characters can coexist in the same party
- Monsters and magic items are unchanged (Monster Manual and DMG revisions are separate products)

### What Is NOT Changed

- The core d20 resolution mechanic (roll d20 + modifiers vs DC/AC)
- Six ability scores and their modifiers
- Proficiency bonus progression (+2 at level 1 to +6 at level 17)
- Hit dice and HP calculation
- Spell slot progression for full, half, and third casters
- The action economy (action, bonus action, reaction, movement)
- Concentration mechanic

title: 2024 Terminology Changes
summary: Race becomes Species, d20 Test replaces ability check/save/attack umbrella, and other terminology shifts in the 2024 PHB.
content:
## 2024 Terminology Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The 2024 PHB introduces several terminology changes to improve clarity and inclusivity.

### Race → Species

The term **Race** is replaced by **Species** throughout the 2024 PHB. Mechanically, species function identically to the old race entries but no longer grant ability score increases (those now come from backgrounds).

- Elf, Dwarf, Halfling, etc. are now **species**
- Half-Elf and Half-Orc are removed as distinct species
- The Ardling (celestial-touched) is a new species option
- Dragonborn, Tiefling, and others receive revised traits

### d20 Test

**d20 Test** is a new umbrella term that encompasses:
- Ability checks
- Attack rolls
- Saving throws

Any rule that says "d20 Test" applies to all three roll types. This simplifies wording for features like the Halfling's **Luck** trait (reroll 1s on any d20 Test) and Bardic Inspiration (add to any d20 Test).

### Bloodied

The **Bloodied** condition is reintroduced from 4th Edition. A creature is Bloodied when it has **half its hit point maximum or fewer**. This is primarily a narrative/communication tool — it tells players and DMs when a creature is significantly wounded. Some features may key off the Bloodied condition.

### Transport

**Transport** replaces the old concept of a mount's movement options. When you mount a creature, you choose whether to control it or let it act independently. The Transport rules clarify how your speed works while mounted.

### Other Terminology Shifts

| 2014 Term | 2024 Term | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Race | Species | No mechanical ASI from species |
| Subrace | Subspecies | Used for some species variants |
| Racial trait | Species trait | Same mechanical function |
| Melee/Ranged weapon attack | Melee/Ranged attack with a weapon | Disambiguates from spell attacks |
| Ability check/save/attack | d20 Test (umbrella) | Individual terms still used |
| Bonus action spell rule | Specific spell timing rules | Clarified per-spell |
| Component pouch/focus | Spellcasting focus | Simplified |
| Proficiency (tools) | Tool proficiency | More explicit |

### Natural 1 and Natural 20

In 2024, **natural 20** on any d20 Test is an automatic success, and **natural 1** is an automatic failure. This extends the critical hit/miss concept from attack rolls to ability checks and saving throws as well. A natural 20 on an ability check grants **Heroic Inspiration** (renamed from Inspiration).

### Heroic Inspiration

**Inspiration** is renamed to **Heroic Inspiration**. You gain it from rolling a natural 20 on any d20 Test. If you already have Heroic Inspiration, you can give it to another player character. Heroic Inspiration lets you reroll any d20 Test and take the new result.

title: 2024 Character Creation Changes
summary: New character creation order (Class, Origin, Abilities), backgrounds grant ASIs, origin feats at level 1, species no longer provide ability score increases.
content:
## 2024 Character Creation Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Character creation has been significantly restructured in the 2024 PHB.

### New Step Order

**2014 order:** Race → Class → Ability Scores → Background → Equipment

**2024 order:**
1. **Choose a Class** — determines hit dice, proficiencies, core features
2. **Determine Origin** — choose Species + Background
3. **Determine Ability Scores** — using standard array, point buy, or rolling
4. **Choose Equipment** — from class/background or starting gold
5. **Fill in Details** — name, appearance, personality

### Backgrounds Grant Ability Scores

The biggest structural change: **backgrounds now grant ability score increases** instead of species.

- Each background provides **+2 to one ability and +1 to another**, or **+1 to three abilities**
- Players choose which abilities to increase (within the background's thematic suggestions)
- Custom backgrounds can assign these bonuses to any abilities
- This decouples mechanical optimization from species choice

### Origin Feats

Every background now grants an **Origin feat** at level 1. Origin feats are a curated list of feats appropriate for starting characters:

- **Alert** — +5 to initiative, can swap initiative with willing ally
- **Crafter** — tool proficiencies, craft items at discount
- **Healer** — use Healer's Kit to restore HP (2d6 + 2 + target's level, once per long rest per creature)
- **Lucky** — 3 luck points per long rest, spend to gain advantage or impose disadvantage
- **Magic Initiate** — 2 cantrips + 1 level 1 spell from a chosen class list
- **Musician** — instrument proficiency, Heroic Inspiration to allies after a rest
- **Savage Attacker** — reroll melee weapon damage once per turn, take higher
- **Skilled** — 3 additional skill or tool proficiencies
- **Tavern Brawler** — enhanced unarmed strikes (1d4), bonus to push/shove
- **Tough** — +2 HP per level (retroactive)

### Species Changes

- Species **no longer grant ability score increases**
- Species provide: creature type, size, speed, special traits (darkvision, resistances, etc.)
- **Half-Elf and Half-Orc are removed** as distinct species
- Players who want a half-elf or half-orc can choose either parent species and flavor accordingly
- Some species traits are revised (e.g., Tiefling gets legacy choices)

### Traits, Ideals, Bonds, and Flaws — Removed

The 2024 PHB **removes** the Traits, Ideals, Bonds, and Flaws system from character creation. These personality elements are still encouraged for roleplay but are no longer a mechanical step with predefined tables.

### Languages

Languages are now granted by **background**, not species. Each background grants **Common plus two other languages** (or one language and one tool proficiency). This means an Elf raised among Dwarves might speak Dwarvish instead of Elvish, reflecting upbringing over biology.

### Alignment

Alignment is de-emphasized. Species no longer have suggested alignments. Alignment remains as an optional descriptor but has no mechanical weight in the 2024 PHB.

title: 2024 Barbarian Changes
summary: Barbarian Rage now lasts 10 minutes, Brutal Strike replaces Brutal Critical, and Reckless Attack works on opportunity attacks.
content:
## 2024 Barbarian Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Barbarian receives quality-of-life improvements and a more interesting high-level progression.

### Rage (Revised)

- Rage now lasts **10 minutes** (no longer ends early if you don't attack or take damage)
- Rage bonus damage: +2 (levels 1–8), +3 (levels 9–15), +4 (levels 16–20)
- At level 1, you can enter Rage as a **bonus action** (unchanged)
- Rage still ends early if you fall unconscious or choose to end it
- Number of Rages: 2 (level 1), 3 (level 3), 4 (level 6), 5 (level 12), 6 (level 17), Unlimited (level 20)

### Brutal Strike (New — Replaces Brutal Critical)

**Brutal Critical** (extra damage dice on crits) is **removed**. In its place:

- **Level 9 — Brutal Strike**: When you use Reckless Attack and hit, you can forgo advantage on the attack to deal an extra **1d10** damage and apply one of these effects:
  - **Forceful Blow**: Push the target 15 feet in a direction you choose. You can then move up to 15 feet toward the target without provoking opportunity attacks.
  - **Hamstring Blow**: Target's speed is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.
- **Level 13**: Brutal Strike damage increases to **2d10**, and you gain a new option:
  - **Staggering Blow**: Target has disadvantage on its next saving throw before the end of your next turn.
- **Level 17**: Brutal Strike damage increases to **3d10**, and you gain:
  - **Sundering Blow**: Before the start of your next turn, the next attack against the target has advantage.

### Reckless Attack (Updated)

- Still grants advantage on STR-based melee attacks on your turn
- Now **also works on opportunity attacks** you make before the start of your next turn
- Enemies still gain advantage on attacks against you until the start of your next turn

### Unarmored Defense (Unchanged)

AC = 10 + DEX modifier + CON modifier (no change from 2014).

### Danger Sense (Updated)

- Now works even if you are **Blinded** (2014 version required you to see the effect)
- Still grants advantage on DEX saving throws against effects you can see

### Primal Knowledge (New)

- At **level 2** and again at **level 9**, you gain proficiency in one skill from the Barbarian list
- Replaces the old feature that granted extra skills at certain subclass levels

### Relentless Rage (Moved)

- Now gained at **level 11** (was level 11 in 2014 as well)
- DC increases by 5 each time you use it, resets on short or long rest (unchanged)

### Persistent Rage (Updated)

- At **level 15**, Rage no longer ends early for any reason except unconsciousness or your choice
- Additionally, if you drop to 0 HP while raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 CON save. On success, drop to 1 HP instead. DC increases by 5 each time.

### Primal Champion (Level 20 — Updated)

- STR and CON increase by 4 each (was 4 each in 2014)
- Maximum for those scores increases to **25** (was 24 in 2014)
- Unlimited Rages

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 10, and 14** (unchanged)
- Path of the Berserker, Path of the Wild Heart (replaces Totem Warrior), Path of the World Tree (new), Path of the Zealot

title: 2024 Bard Changes
summary: Bard gains Expertise at level 2, Magical Secrets is unrestricted, and weapon proficiencies are reduced.
content:
## 2024 Bard Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Bard receives focused refinements that sharpen its identity as the ultimate support and skill class.

### Bardic Inspiration (Updated)

- Still a bonus action to grant an ally a Bardic Inspiration die
- Die sizes: d6 (level 1), d8 (level 5), d10 (level 10), d12 (level 15)
- Uses equal to **Charisma modifier** (minimum 1), regained on a **long rest**
- At **level 5** (Font of Inspiration), regain uses on a **short or long rest**
- The recipient can add the die to **any d20 Test** (attack, check, or save) — this is broadened by the d20 Test terminology
- Unused Bardic Inspiration die now expires after **10 minutes** (was 10 minutes in 2014, unchanged)

### Expertise (Moved to Level 2)

- Expertise is now gained at **level 2** (was level 3 in 2014)
- Choose 2 skill proficiencies to gain Expertise (double proficiency bonus)
- Additional 2 Expertise choices at **level 9** (was level 10)

### Weapon Proficiencies (Reduced)

- 2014: Simple weapons, hand crossbows, longswords, rapiers, shortswords
- 2024: **Simple weapons only**
- Bards who want martial weapon proficiency can take the Warrior origin feat or multiclass

### Jack of All Trades (Level 2 — Unchanged)

- Add half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) to ability checks you're not proficient in

### Magical Secrets (Revised)

- In 2014, Magical Secrets was gained at levels 10, 14, and 18, letting you learn 2 spells from any class list each time
- In 2024, the Bard's **spell list is expanded** to include many former Magical Secrets staples
- **Magical Secrets** now works differently: at **level 11**, when you prepare spells, you can include spells from **any class's spell list** (not just the Bard list), as long as you have spell slots of the appropriate level
- This is more flexible — you can swap these choices each day when you prepare spells

### Spellcasting Changes

- Bards are now **prepared casters** (not known casters)
- Number of prepared spells = Bard level + CHA modifier
- You can change your prepared spells after a **long rest**
- This is a major change from the 2014 Bard, who learned a fixed number of spells and could only swap one per level-up

### Song of Rest (Removed)

- **Song of Rest is removed** from the 2024 Bard
- This was a signature Bard feature in 2014 (extra healing during short rests)

### Countercharm (Removed)

- **Countercharm is removed** from the 2024 Bard
- Was rarely used in 2014 (advantage on saves vs charm/fright while you performed)

### Words of Creation (New — Level 7)

- When you use Bardic Inspiration and the recipient fails their d20 Test, the Bardic Inspiration die is **not expended**
- This makes Bardic Inspiration significantly more reliable

### Superior Inspiration (Updated — Level 18)

- At initiative, if you have no Bardic Inspiration uses remaining, you regain **2 uses** (was 1 in 2014)

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 10, and 14**
- College of Dance (new), College of Glamour, College of Lore, College of Valor

title: 2024 Cleric Changes
summary: Cleric gains Divine Order at level 1 for martial or caster focus, domains are consolidated, and Channel Divinity is revised.
content:
## 2024 Cleric Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Cleric receives structural changes that provide more customization at level 1 and streamline domain options.

### Divine Order (New — Level 1)

At level 1, Clerics choose a **Divine Order** that shapes their playstyle:

- **Protector**: Gain proficiency with **martial weapons and heavy armor**. This replaces the 2014 approach where only certain domains granted heavy armor.
- **Thaumaturge**: Gain one extra **cantrip** from the Cleric spell list. Add your WIS modifier to the damage of your Cleric cantrips.

This choice is independent of your subclass (domain), allowing any domain to be either martial or caster-focused.

### Channel Divinity (Updated)

- Uses: **2** at level 1 (was 1 in 2014 at level 2), **3** at level 6
- Regained on a **short or long rest** (unchanged)
- **Turn Undead** is still a default Channel Divinity option
- **Divine Spark** (new): As an action, either deal radiant damage (1d8 per Channel Divinity use) to one creature within 30 feet (CON save), or restore hit points equal to the same amount to one creature within 30 feet
- Destroy Undead is now part of Turn Undead progression (CR threshold increases with level)

### Domain Consolidation

The 2024 PHB consolidates domains:

- **Life Domain** — retained, revised
- **Light Domain** — retained, revised
- **Trickery Domain** — retained, revised
- **War Domain** — retained, revised

Domains removed from the PHB (may appear in other sourcebooks): Knowledge, Nature, Tempest, Forge, Grave, Order, Peace, Twilight, Arcana, Death.

### Smite Undead (New — Level 5)

- When you use Turn Undead and the undead fails its save, you also deal radiant damage to it: **1d8 per Cleric level** divided as you choose among affected undead

### Blessed Strikes (Level 7 — Revised)

- **Divine Strike**: Once per turn when you hit with a weapon attack, deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage (scales to 2d8 at level 14)
- **Potent Spellcasting**: Add WIS modifier to Cleric cantrip damage
- You choose one of these at level 7 (unchanged from Tasha's variant)

### Divine Intervention (Level 10 — Revised)

- No longer a percentile roll. Instead, you can cast any **Cleric spell of 5th level or lower** without expending a spell slot, and without needing material components
- Usable **once per long rest**
- At **level 20** (Greater Divine Intervention): cast any Cleric spell of **9th level or lower** in this way

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 10, and 14** (was 1, 2, 6, 8, 17 in 2014)
- This is a significant structural change — domain choice is no longer at level 1
- Domain is now chosen at **level 3** like most other classes

title: 2024 Druid Changes
summary: Druid Wild Shape now uses a pool-based template system instead of stat blocks, and the Circle of the Sea is a new subclass.
content:
## 2024 Druid Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Druid receives a comprehensive Wild Shape overhaul and new subclass options.

### Wild Shape (Major Overhaul)

The entire Wild Shape system is redesigned:

**2014 approach**: Transform into a beast whose stat block you've seen. You use the beast's stats entirely.

**2024 approach**: Wild Shape uses **template forms** instead of specific beast stat blocks.

#### How 2024 Wild Shape Works

- Use a **bonus action** to assume a Wild Shape form (unchanged)
- Uses: **2 per short or long rest** at level 1
- Duration: **1 hour per Druid level** (significantly increased from 2014's level/2 hours)
- You gain **temporary hit points** instead of replacing your HP: equal to your Druid level × the form's Temp HP multiplier
- You use your own ability scores, proficiency bonus, and proficiencies
- Your attacks use your spellcasting modifier (WIS) for attack and damage rolls

#### Template Forms

At level 1, you can assume these forms:
- **Animal of the Land**: Speed 40 ft, climb 40 ft. Multiattack (2 strikes). Strike deals 1d8 + WIS modifier.
- **Animal of the Sea**: Swim 60 ft. Multiattack (2 strikes). Strike deals 1d6 + WIS modifier. Can breathe underwater.
- **Animal of the Sky** (available at level 4): Fly 60 ft. Strike deals 1d6 + WIS modifier.

Damage and temp HP scale with Druid level.

### Spellcasting in Wild Shape

- At **level 2**, you can cast spells while in Wild Shape (2014 required Moon Druid for this at level 2, and other Druids at level 18)
- This is a massive quality-of-life improvement for all Druid subclasses

### Primal Order (New — Level 1)

Similar to the Cleric's Divine Order:
- **Magician**: Learn one extra cantrip from the Druid list
- **Warden**: Gain proficiency with martial weapons and medium armor, and your unarmed strike damage increases

### Wild Companion (Moved to Core)

- Spend a Wild Shape use to cast *Find Familiar* without material components
- Now a base Druid feature instead of a Tasha's optional feature

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 10, and 14** (was level 2 in 2014)
- **Circle of the Land** — revised, terrain-based spell lists
- **Circle of the Moon** — still focused on enhanced Wild Shape combat
- **Circle of the Sea** (new) — water and storm-themed abilities
- **Circle of the Stars** — retained from Tasha's, revised

### Metal Armor Restriction

- The 2024 PHB **removes** the traditional prohibition on Druids wearing metal armor. Druids can now wear any armor they're proficient with regardless of material.

title: 2024 Fighter Changes
summary: Fighter gains the Weapon Mastery system with 8 properties, Second Wind is improved, and fighting styles become feats.
content:
## 2024 Fighter Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Fighter gains the headline Weapon Mastery system and several quality-of-life improvements.

### Weapon Mastery (New — Core Feature)

The Fighter's signature new feature. At **level 1**, the Fighter can use **Weapon Mastery properties** on weapons they wield.

- The Fighter gains mastery with **3 weapons** at level 1, increasing to **4** at level 4, **5** at level 10, and **6** at level 16
- Each weapon has a fixed Mastery property (see the `2024-weapon-mastery` article for full details)
- When you attack with a weapon you have mastery over, you can use its Mastery property
- You can change your mastery weapon choices when you finish a **long rest**

See the dedicated **Weapon Mastery** article for the 8 properties: Cleave, Graze, Nick, Push, Sap, Slow, Topple, Vex.

### Fighting Style → Feat

- Fighting styles are no longer class features — they are now **feats**
- The Fighter gains a **bonus feat** at level 1 that can be a Fighting Style feat
- Fighting Style feats: Archery (+2 to ranged attack rolls), Defense (+1 AC in armor), Dueling (+2 damage with one-handed weapon and no other weapon), Great Weapon Fighting (reroll 1s and 2s on damage), Protection (impose disadvantage on attacks against nearby allies with shield), Two-Weapon Fighting (add ability modifier to off-hand damage), Thrown Weapon Fighting (+2 damage with thrown weapons, draw as part of attack), Blind Fighting (10 ft blindsight), Interception (reduce damage to nearby ally)

### Second Wind (Improved)

- Still a bonus action to regain HP: **1d10 + Fighter level**
- Now usable a number of times equal to your **proficiency bonus** per short or long rest (was 1/short rest in 2014)
- At level 2 with +2 proficiency, that's 2 uses. At level 17 with +6, it's 6 uses.

### Action Surge (Updated)

- Still grants an extra action on your turn
- **1 use** at level 2, **2 uses** at level 17
- Recharges on a **short or long rest** (unchanged)

### Tactical Mind (New — Level 2)

- When you fail an ability check, you can expend a **Second Wind use** to add **1d10** to the roll, potentially changing the result
- This gives Fighters out-of-combat utility with their Second Wind resource

### Indomitable (Updated)

- Still lets you reroll a failed saving throw
- Uses: **1** at level 9, **2** at level 13, **3** at level 17
- If the reroll still fails, you gain **temporary HP** equal to your Fighter level (new consolation benefit)

### Extra Attack Progression

- Extra Attack at level 5 (2 attacks), level 11 (3 attacks), level 20 (4 attacks)
- Unchanged from 2014

### Studied Attacks (New — Level 13)

- When you miss a creature with an attack, you have **advantage** on your next attack against that creature before the end of your next turn

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 7, 10, 15, and 18**
- Battle Master, Champion, Eldritch Knight, Psi Warrior

title: 2024 Monk Changes
summary: Monk receives a significant overhaul making it much stronger, with reworked Ki (now Focus Points), improved martial arts die progression, and better features at every tier.
content:
## 2024 Monk Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Monk receives the most significant overhaul of any class in the 2024 PHB, addressing longstanding community concerns about the class's power level.

### Ki → Focus Points (Renamed & Improved)

- **Ki points** are renamed to **Focus Points** (or Discipline Points in some printings)
- Pool size: **equal to Monk level** (unchanged)
- Regained on a **short or long rest** (unchanged)
- Key improvement: several features that previously cost Ki are now **free**

### Martial Arts (Improved)

- Martial Arts die progression is **accelerated**:
  - 2014: d4 (1), d6 (5), d8 (11), d10 (17)
  - 2024: **d6 (1), d8 (5), d10 (11), d12 (17)**
- Unarmed strikes and Monk weapons can use DEX for attack and damage (unchanged)
- **Bonus unarmed strike** after attacking with a Monk weapon or unarmed strike (unchanged)

### Flurry of Blows (Updated)

- Spend **1 Focus Point** as a bonus action to make **2 unarmed strikes** (unchanged base function)
- At **level 10**, Flurry of Blows improves to **3 unarmed strikes**

### Patient Defense (Free Option Added)

- **Free version**: Take the Dodge action as a bonus action (no Focus Point cost)
- **Enhanced version**: Spend 1 Focus Point to Dodge as a bonus action AND gain the ability to make an extra unarmed strike
- In 2014, Patient Defense always cost 1 Ki point

### Step of the Wind (Free Option Added)

- **Free version**: Take the Disengage action as a bonus action and your jump distance doubles for the turn
- **Enhanced version**: Spend 1 Focus Point to also Dash as part of the same bonus action
- In 2014, Step of the Wind always cost 1 Ki point

### Deflect Attacks (Improved — Replaces Deflect Missiles)

- Now works against **all attack damage types**, not just ranged missile attacks
- Reduce damage by **1d10 + DEX modifier + Monk level**
- If reduced to 0, you can spend **1 Focus Point** to make a ranged attack (as before with Deflect Missiles)
- This is a massive improvement — Monks can now deflect sword swings, fire breath, etc.

### Uncanny Metabolism (New — Level 2)

- When you roll initiative, you can regain **all spent Focus Points** (once per long rest)
- Also regain HP equal to your **Martial Arts die + Monk level**
- Ensures Monks start every combat with full resources

### Stunning Strike (Updated — Level 5)

- Spend **1 Focus Point** when you hit with an unarmed strike or Monk weapon
- Target must make a **CON save** against your Focus save DC
- On failure: **Stunned until the start of your next turn** (unchanged)
- New limitation: Can only use once per turn (prevents the 2014 strategy of burning all Ki on Stunning Strike spam)

### Heightened Focus (New — Level 10)

- Flurry of Blows: 3 unarmed strikes instead of 2
- Patient Defense: When you spend a Focus Point, you also gain temporary HP equal to your Martial Arts die × 2
- Step of the Wind: When you spend a Focus Point, your speed doubles for the turn

### Self-Restoration (New — Level 10)

- At the end of your turn, you can end one condition on yourself: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned
- No action or Focus Point cost

### Perfect Discipline (Level 15)

- When you roll initiative and have fewer Focus Points remaining than your proficiency bonus, regain Focus Points to equal your proficiency bonus

### Body and Mind (Level 20 — Capstone)

- DEX and WIS increase by 4 each
- Maximum for those scores increases to **25**

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 11, and 17**
- Way of Mercy, Way of Shadow, Way of the Four Elements (revised), Way of the Open Hand
tags: rules,2024,5e-2024,monk,focus points,ki,martial arts,deflect attacks,stunning strike,class changes

title: 2024 Paladin Changes
summary: Divine Smite is now a bonus action spell (no crit smiting), spell slots round up for half-caster progression, and auras are improved.
content:
## 2024 Paladin Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Paladin receives changes aimed at balancing the class's damage output while improving its support capabilities.

### Divine Smite — Now a Spell

The single most impactful change to any class feature:

- **2014**: Divine Smite was a class feature. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you could expend a spell slot to deal extra radiant damage (2d8 for a 1st-level slot, +1d8 per slot level above 1st, max 5d8, +1d8 vs undead/fiends).
- **2024**: **Divine Smite is now a 1st-level Paladin spell** with these properties:
  - **Bonus action** casting time
  - Must be cast **before** you make the attack (you declare it, then attack)
  - Deals radiant damage on a hit: 2d8 + 1d8 per slot level above 1st
  - Maximum damage cap: **5d8** (at a 4th-level slot)
  - +1d8 against Fiends and Undead (unchanged)
  - **Concentration, up to 1 minute**
  - Requires a **melee weapon attack on the next turn**

#### Critical Smite — Removed

Because Divine Smite is now a spell declared before the attack, you can **no longer choose to smite after seeing a critical hit**. This was the Paladin's most powerful combat interaction in 2014 ("crit fishing" with advantage and smiting on crits for double damage dice). This is intentionally removed.

### Spell Slot Progression (Half-Caster Fix)

- 2024 half-casters (Paladin, Ranger) now **round up** when calculating spell slots from the multiclass table
- At level 1, Paladins gain **spell slots** and **spellcasting** immediately (was level 2 in 2014)
- Paladins prepare spells from the Paladin list: number = Paladin level + CHA modifier

### Lay on Hands (Updated)

- Still a pool of healing equal to **Paladin level × 5 HP**
- Now used as a **bonus action** (was an action in 2014)
- Can still spend 5 HP from the pool to cure one disease or neutralize one poison

### Channel Divinity (Updated)

- Gained at **level 3** with subclass
- **2 uses** per short or long rest at level 3, **3 uses** at level 11
- **Divine Sense** is now a Channel Divinity option (was a separate feature in 2014)

### Aura of Protection (Updated — Level 6)

- All allies (and you) within **10 feet** add your CHA modifier to saving throws (unchanged)
- Range increases to **30 feet** at level 18 (was 30 feet at level 18 in 2014, unchanged)
- Now explicitly works while you are **incapacitated** (2014 specified you had to be conscious)

### Aura of Courage (Level 10 — Updated)

- You and allies within your aura are **immune to the Frightened condition** (was "can't be frightened" in 2014, functionally identical)

### Radiant Strikes (New — Level 11)

- Replaces Improved Divine Smite
- Your weapon attacks deal an extra **1d8 radiant damage** on a hit (unchanged mechanically from Improved Divine Smite)

### Restoring Touch (New — Level 14)

- When you use Lay on Hands, you can also end one of these conditions: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned
- No extra HP cost from the pool

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 7, 15, and 20**
- Oath of Devotion, Oath of the Ancients, Oath of Vengeance, Oath of Glory
tags: rules,2024,5e-2024,paladin,divine smite,spell,crit smite,aura,lay on hands,class changes

title: 2024 Ranger Changes
summary: Ranger receives revised core features, spell slots round up for half-caster progression, and Favored Enemy and Natural Explorer are replaced.
content:
## 2024 Ranger Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Ranger receives substantial revisions that address the class's widely acknowledged weaknesses in the 2014 edition.

### Favored Enemy (Replaced)

- **2014 Favored Enemy**: Choose a creature type, gain advantage on tracking and recalling information about them. Widely considered one of the weakest class features in the game.
- **2024 Replacement — Favored Enemy**: Now grants **Hunter's Mark** as a prepared spell that doesn't count against your prepared spells total. You can cast it **without concentration** at level 1, and the number of free casts (no spell slot) increases with level.
  - This is a massive improvement — Hunter's Mark is always available and doesn't compete with concentration spells

### Natural Explorer (Replaced)

- **2014 Natural Explorer**: Choose a terrain type, gain various exploration benefits in that terrain. Too situational.
- **2024 Replacement — Deft Explorer**: Gain **Expertise** in one skill at level 1. This replaces the terrain-dependent benefits with a universally useful feature.

### Spellcasting (Improved)

- Rangers are now **prepared casters** (was known casters in 2014)
- Number of prepared spells = Ranger level + WIS modifier
- Can change prepared spells after a **long rest**
- Spell slots **round up** for half-caster progression
- Spellcasting begins at **level 1** (was level 2 in 2014)

### Weapon Mastery

- Rangers gain **Weapon Mastery** with **2 weapons** at level 1
- This gives Rangers access to the new mastery properties alongside Fighters

### Fighting Style → Feat (Level 2)

- Like the Fighter, the Ranger gains a bonus feat at level 2 that can be a Fighting Style feat

### Extra Attack (Level 5)

- Unchanged from 2014: make 2 attacks when you take the Attack action

### Roving (Level 6)

- Speed increases by **10 feet** while not wearing heavy armor
- Gain a **climb speed and swim speed** equal to your walking speed

### Tireless (Level 10)

- As a bonus action, gain **temporary HP** equal to 1d8 + WIS modifier (usable a number of times equal to proficiency bonus per long rest)
- When you finish a short rest, reduce your **Exhaustion level** by 1

### Nature's Veil (Level 14)

- As a bonus action, become **invisible** until the start of your next turn
- Usable a number of times equal to proficiency bonus per long rest

### Feral Senses (Level 18)

- Gain **Blindsight** out to 30 feet
- Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn't behind total cover, even if you're Blinded or in darkness

### Foe Slayer (Level 20 — Capstone)

- Once per turn when you hit a creature marked by Hunter's Mark, you deal extra damage equal to **1d10 + WIS modifier**
- Hunter's Mark no longer requires concentration for you (if not already granted by earlier features)

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 7, 11, and 15**
- Beast Master (revised), Fey Wanderer, Gloom Stalker, Hunter

title: 2024 Rogue Changes
summary: Rogue gains Cunning Strike options that trade Sneak Attack dice for effects, and Sneak Attack now works once per turn (not once per round).
content:
## 2024 Rogue Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Rogue gains new tactical options through Cunning Strike and receives clarified Sneak Attack rules.

### Sneak Attack (Clarified)

- Damage progression unchanged: 1d6 at level 1, scaling by 1d6 every 2 levels to 10d6 at level 19
- **Critical change in wording**: Sneak Attack now explicitly states **once per turn** (not once per round)
- In practice, this means you can Sneak Attack on your turn AND on a reaction attack (such as an opportunity attack) in the same round
- 2014 wording was ambiguous on this point; the 2024 wording makes it intentional

### Cunning Strike (New — Level 5)

When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can **forgo some Sneak Attack dice** to apply one of these effects instead:

- **Poison** (cost: 1d6): Target must make a CON save against your Cunning Strike DC (8 + DEX mod + proficiency) or be **Poisoned** for 1 minute. Target repeats the save at end of each turn.
- **Trip** (cost: 1d6): Target must make a DEX save or be knocked **Prone**.
- **Withdraw** (cost: 1d6): You can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks immediately after dealing damage.

### Additional Cunning Strike Options (Higher Levels)

- **Level 9 — Daze** (cost: 2d6): Target must make a CON save or be **Dazed** (can't take bonus actions or reactions) until the end of its next turn.
- **Level 13 — Knock Out** (cost: 6d6): Target must make a CON save or be **Unconscious** for 1 minute (or until it takes damage or someone uses an action to wake it). Only works if the target is surprised or if you have advantage on the attack.
- **Level 13 — Obscure** (cost: 3d6): Target must make a DEX save or be **Blinded** until the end of its next turn.

### Weapon Mastery

- Rogues gain **Weapon Mastery** with **2 weapons** at level 1
- Great synergy with the **Nick** and **Vex** mastery properties

### Steady Aim (Removed)

- The Tasha's Cauldron optional feature **Steady Aim** is not included in the 2024 Rogue

### Cunning Action (Updated — Level 2)

- Still allows Dash, Disengage, or Hide as a bonus action
- The **Hide** action now follows the new stealth rules (base DC 15)

### Reliable Talent (Level 7 — Moved)

- Treat any d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10 on ability checks where you have proficiency
- Moved from level 11 to **level 7** — a significant buff to the Rogue's skill reliability

### Evasion (Level 7)

- Unchanged: DEX saves for half damage become no damage on success, half on failure

### Subtle Strikes (New — Level 9)

- When you attack, if neither you nor your target has advantage or disadvantage, you can treat the attack as having advantage for the purpose of Sneak Attack eligibility
- This makes Sneak Attack more reliable without needing an ally adjacent

### Slippery Mind (Level 15)

- Gain proficiency in WIS and CHA saving throws (was WIS saves only in 2014 at level 15)

### Elusive (Level 18)

- No attack roll against you has advantage while you aren't incapacitated (unchanged)

### Stroke of Luck (Level 20)

- Turn a miss into a hit, or treat a failed ability check as a natural 20 (1/short or long rest)

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 9, 13, and 17**
- Arcane Trickster, Assassin, Soul Knife, Thief

title: 2024 Sorcerer Changes
summary: Sorcerer gains Innate Sorcery feature, sorcery points and spell slots are merged into a unified system, and the class is significantly buffed.
content:
## 2024 Sorcerer Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Sorcerer receives major buffs that address its historically underpowered status compared to other full casters.

### Innate Sorcery (New — Level 1)

- As a bonus action, you can activate **Innate Sorcery** for 1 minute
- While active:
  - Your spell save DC increases by **1**
  - You have **advantage** on attack rolls for Sorcerer spells
- Usable **2 times per long rest**
- This gives the Sorcerer a unique combat activation that no other caster has

### Sorcery Points (Revised)

- Pool size: **equal to Sorcerer level** (unchanged)
- Regained on a **long rest** (unchanged)
- The conversion between sorcery points and spell slots is **streamlined**:
  - Creating a spell slot: costs a number of sorcery points equal to the slot's level (1 point for 1st, 2 for 2nd, etc.)
  - Converting a spell slot to points: gain sorcery points equal to the slot's level
  - No more lookup table — it's a direct 1:1 relationship between slot level and point cost
- Maximum spell slot you can create: **5th level** (unchanged)

### Font of Magic (Level 2 — Updated)

- You learn to tap into your Sorcery Points
- At **level 2**, you can convert spell slots to sorcery points and vice versa (moved from level 2, unchanged)

### Metamagic (Updated)

- Learn **2 Metamagic options** at level 2, another at levels 9 and 17
- You can use **only one Metamagic option per spell** unless stated otherwise (unchanged)

#### Metamagic Options (Revised)

- **Careful Spell** (1 point): Chosen creatures automatically succeed on the spell's save
- **Distant Spell** (1 point): Double range, or 30 ft if touch
- **Empowered Spell** (1 point): Reroll up to CHA modifier damage dice, take new results. Can combine with other Metamagic.
- **Extended Spell** (1 point): Double duration, max 24 hours
- **Heightened Spell** (2 points): One target has **disadvantage** on first save
- **Quickened Spell** (2 points): Change casting time from 1 action to 1 **bonus action**
- **Seeking Spell** (1 point): Reroll the attack roll for a spell (new)
- **Subtle Spell** (1 point): No verbal or somatic components
- **Transmuted Spell** (1 point): Change damage type to acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, or thunder
- **Twinned Spell**: **REMOVED** from the 2024 PHB (was one of the most powerful and contentious Metamagic options)

### Sorcerous Restoration (Level 5 — New)

- When you finish a **short rest**, you regain **sorcery points equal to half your Sorcerer level** (rounded up)
- This is a major resource improvement, addressing the Sorcerer's 2014 problem of running out of resources quickly

### Sorcery Incarnate (New — Level 13)

- While Innate Sorcery is active, you can use **two Metamagic options** on a single spell (was always limited to one)
- If you have no uses of Innate Sorcery left, you can spend **2 sorcery points** to activate it again

### Arcane Apotheosis (New — Level 18)

- While Innate Sorcery is active, you can use one of your Metamagic options at a **reduced cost** (1 fewer sorcery point, minimum 0)

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 14, and 18**
- Aberrant Sorcery (revised Mind Flayer theme), Clockwork Sorcery, Draconic Sorcery, Wild Magic Sorcery

title: 2024 Warlock Changes
summary: Warlock gains Pact Boon at level 1, has flexible spell slot recovery, and Eldritch Invocations are restructured.
content:
## 2024 Warlock Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Warlock receives structural changes that make the class more flexible and front-load its identity-defining features.

### Pact Boon (Moved to Level 1)

- In 2014, Pact Boon was gained at **level 3**
- In 2024, Pact Boon is gained at **level 1**, defining your Warlock identity from the start

#### Pact Boons

- **Pact of the Blade**: Gain proficiency with martial weapons. You can conjure a pact weapon as a bonus action — it counts as magical, and you can use CHA for attack and damage rolls. The weapon can be any melee weapon form.
- **Pact of the Chain**: Learn *Find Familiar* and can cast it without material components. Your familiar gains enhanced forms (Imp, Pseudodragon, Quasit, Sprite, or a special Pact familiar). When you take the Attack action, you can forgo one attack to let your familiar attack instead.
- **Pact of the Tome**: Gain a Book of Shadows containing **3 cantrips** from any class list. You also gain a ritual casting feature: you can transcribe ritual spells into your book and cast them as rituals.

### Spellcasting (Revised)

- Warlocks still use **Pact Magic** with limited spell slots that recharge on a **short or long rest**
- Spell slot progression:
  - Levels 1–2: 1 slot (1st level)
  - Levels 3–4: 2 slots (2nd level)
  - Levels 5–6: 2 slots (3rd level)
  - Levels 7–8: 2 slots (4th level)
  - Levels 9–10: 2 slots (5th level)
  - Levels 11+: 3 slots (5th level)
- At **level 11**, Warlocks gain a **3rd Pact Magic slot** (was 3 at level 11 in 2014, unchanged)
- **Mystic Arcanum** still provides one casting each of 6th, 7th, 8th, and 9th level spells (gained at levels 11, 13, 15, 17)

### Magical Cunning (New — Level 2)

- When you have **no Pact Magic spell slots** remaining, you can perform a 1-minute ritual to regain **half your maximum Pact Magic slots** (rounded up)
- Usable **once per long rest**
- This helps address the Warlock's issue of running dry between short rests in parties that don't take short rests

### Eldritch Invocations (Restructured)

- Still gained at level 2, with additional choices at higher levels
- Total invocations known: starts at 2, increases incrementally to 8 at level 18
- Many invocations are revised:
  - **Agonizing Blast**: Add CHA to Eldritch Blast damage (unchanged)
  - **Armor of Shadows**: Cast *Mage Armor* on yourself at will (unchanged)
  - **Devil's Sight**: See normally in magical and nonmagical darkness to 120 ft (unchanged)
  - **Eldritch Spear**: Eldritch Blast range becomes 300 ft (revised)
  - **Lifedrinker**: Once per turn when you hit with your pact weapon or unarmed strike, deal extra necrotic damage equal to your proficiency bonus (revised, was CHA modifier)
  - **Thirsting Blade**: Attack twice with pact weapon when you take the Attack action (unchanged)
- Some invocations that were pact-specific are now open to all Warlocks
- Invocations can be swapped when you gain a Warlock level (unchanged)

### Contact Patron (New — Level 9)

- You can cast *Contact Other Plane* to contact your patron without expending a spell slot
- When you do so, you **automatically succeed** on the INT save (no risk of madness)
- Usable once per long rest

### Eldritch Master (Level 20 — Revised)

- Once per long rest, you can spend 1 minute to regain **all expended Pact Magic slots**
- Additionally, when you use Magical Cunning, you regain all slots (not half)

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 10, and 14**
- Archfey Patron, Celestial Patron, Fiend Patron, Great Old One Patron

title: 2024 Wizard Changes
summary: Wizard receives relatively minor changes: adjusted spell preparation formula, Scholar feature, and Memorize Spell replaces Signature Spells.
content:
## 2024 Wizard Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

The Wizard receives the fewest changes of any class, as it was already considered one of the strongest and most well-designed classes in 2014.

### Spell Preparation (Adjusted)

- Number of prepared spells = **Wizard level + INT modifier** (unchanged formula)
- Wizards remain **prepared casters** who prepare from their spellbook
- The Wizard's spellbook still functions the same way: copy spells found in the world into your book at a cost of 50 gp and 2 hours per spell level

### Academic (New — Level 1, replaces Arcane Recovery partially)

- Gain **Expertise** in one of your skill proficiencies (must be a skill you're proficient in)
- This gives the Wizard a non-magical utility feature from the start

### Arcane Recovery (Level 1 — Updated)

- Once per long rest, at the end of a **short rest**, recover spell slots with a combined level equal to **half your Wizard level** (rounded up)
- No single slot can be 6th level or higher (unchanged)
- Functionally similar to 2014, but the wording and timing are clarified

### Memorize Spell (New — Level 5)

- As a bonus action, you can change **one prepared spell** to another spell in your spellbook
- The new spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots
- Usable once, and you regain the use after finishing a short or long rest
- This gives Wizards mid-day flexibility that was previously impossible

### Spell Mastery (Level 18 — Updated)

- Choose **1 first-level spell and 1 second-level spell** from your spellbook
- You can cast these at their base level **without expending a spell slot**
- You can change your choices when you finish a long rest
- In 2014, you had to spend 8 hours to change your Spell Mastery choices; now it just requires a long rest

### Signature Spells (Level 20 — Revised)

- Choose **2 third-level spells** from your spellbook
- You always have them prepared (they don't count against your prepared spells limit)
- You can cast each one once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot, regaining the uses on a short or long rest
- This is similar to the 2014 version but clarified

### Cantrip Changes

- Wizards can swap **one cantrip** for another from the Wizard list whenever they gain a Wizard level
- This is a new flexibility option (2014 Wizards were stuck with their cantrip choices)

### Subclass Structure

- Subclass features at levels **3, 6, 10, and 14** (was level 2 in 2014)
- Abjurer, Diviner, Evoker, Illusionist
- School specialization is now chosen at level 3 instead of level 2
- Only 4 subclasses in the 2024 PHB (down from 8 in the 2014 PHB)

### Minor Changes

- The Wizard's **ritual casting** now follows the general ritual casting rules (see Spellcasting Changes article)
- **Saving throw proficiencies**: INT and WIS (unchanged)
- **Hit dice**: d6 (unchanged)
- **Armor/weapon proficiencies**: None/daggers, darts, slings, quarterstaffs, light crossbows (unchanged)

title: 2024 Combat Rule Changes
summary: Surprise now imposes disadvantage on initiative, opportunity attacks are revised, unarmed strikes use Dexterity saves, and two-weapon fighting is expanded.
content:
## 2024 Combat Rule Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Combat receives multiple quality-of-life changes and mechanical revisions.

### Surprise (Overhauled)

**2014**: Surprised creatures cannot move, take actions, bonus actions, or reactions for the first round. An entire "surprise round" effectively exists.

**2024**: There is **no surprise round**. Instead:
- Surprised creatures have **disadvantage on their initiative roll**
- Once initiative is rolled, combat proceeds normally — a "surprised" creature can still act on its turn
- This means high-initiative creatures can still act quickly even when surprised
- Surprised creatures **cannot take Reactions** until their first turn ends (retained from 2014)

This is a significant nerf to Assassin Rogues and ambush strategies, but it eliminates the awkward "you're surprised so you skip your entire turn" mechanic.

### Opportunity Attacks (Revised)

- Still triggered when a creature you can see **leaves your reach**
- Key change: Opportunity attacks are now triggered only by **willing movement**
- Being **pushed, pulled, or teleported** out of reach does NOT provoke opportunity attacks
- The Disengage action still prevents opportunity attacks
- You still use your **Reaction** to make an opportunity attack

### Unarmed Strikes (Revised)

- Base unarmed strike damage: **1 + STR modifier** (unchanged)
- New options when you make an unarmed strike, you can choose:
  - **Damage**: Deal 1 + STR modifier bludgeoning damage (standard)
  - **Grapple**: Target must succeed on a **STR or DEX save** (its choice) against your Grapple DC (8 + STR mod + proficiency) or be grappled. The grappled creature's speed becomes 0 and it can use its action to repeat the save.
  - **Shove**: Target must succeed on a **STR or DEX save** (its choice) against your Shove DC (same as Grapple DC) or be pushed **5 feet** away from you or knocked **Prone**
- Grapple and Shove now use **saves** instead of contested checks — this is faster to resolve

### Two-Weapon Fighting (Expanded)

**2014**: When you take the Attack action with a light melee weapon, you can use your bonus action to attack with a different light melee weapon in the other hand. Don't add ability modifier to damage.

**2024**:
- When you take the Attack action and attack with a **Light weapon**, you can make one extra attack as part of the same action (NOT as a bonus action) with a different Light weapon
- You **do add** your ability modifier to the off-hand attack's damage
- This frees up the bonus action for other uses (spells, class features, etc.)
- The Two-Weapon Fighting feat is no longer needed to add the ability modifier

### Drawing/Stowing Weapons

- You can draw or stow **one weapon** as part of each attack you make (if you have Extra Attack, you can draw/stow with each individual attack)
- This resolves the 2014 ambiguity about action economy for weapon swapping

### Movement Through Allies

- You can move through a **friendly creature's space** freely (unchanged from 2014)
- You **cannot end your turn** in an ally's space unless you are Tiny or the ally is at least two sizes larger
- Clarified that moving through an **enemy's space** requires it to be at least two sizes larger or smaller than you

### Influence Action (New)

- A new standard action that consolidates several social interactions
- You can make a **CHA check** (Deception, Intimidation, Performance, or Persuasion) contested by the target's **Insight** check
- On success, the target becomes **Charmed** by you until the end of your next turn, or you gain some other social benefit as adjudicated by the DM

### Study Action (New)

- A new standard action to learn about a creature
- Make an **INT check** (Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion) against a DC set by the DM
- On success, you learn one piece of information: resistances, immunities, vulnerabilities, or a similar trait

### Help Action (Revised)

- When you take the Help action to assist an ally's ability check, the ally must make the check **before the start of your next turn**
- When you use Help to assist an attack, you must be within **5 feet** of the target (not the ally), and the ally gains advantage on the next attack against that target before the start of your next turn

title: 2024 Weapon Mastery Properties
summary: Full breakdown of the 8 weapon mastery properties (Cleave, Graze, Nick, Push, Sap, Slow, Topple, Vex), which weapons have them, and how classes interact with mastery.
content:
## 2024 Weapon Mastery Properties

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Weapon Mastery is one of the biggest new systems in the 2024 PHB. Each weapon has a **Mastery property** that martial characters can unlock.

### How Weapon Mastery Works

1. Certain classes grant you mastery with a specific number of weapons
2. When you attack with a weapon you have mastery over, you can use its Mastery property
3. You choose which weapons you have mastery with, and can change your choices after a **long rest**
4. Mastery properties trigger automatically on a hit (or miss, for Graze) — no action cost

### The 8 Mastery Properties

#### Cleave
**Effect**: When you hit a creature, you can make an attack roll against a **second creature within 5 feet** of the first, within your reach. On a hit, the second creature takes the weapon's damage, but **don't add your ability modifier** to the damage (unless the modifier is negative). You can use Cleave only once per turn.

**Weapons**: Greataxe, Halberd

#### Graze
**Effect**: When you **miss** with an attack, you still deal damage equal to the **ability modifier** you used for the attack roll (minimum 0). This damage is the same type the weapon normally deals.

**Weapons**: Glaive, Greatsword

#### Nick
**Effect**: When you make the extra attack from the **Light weapon property** (two-weapon fighting), you can make that extra attack as part of the Attack action instead of using a bonus action. You can still make only one extra attack in this way per turn.

**Weapons**: Dagger, Light Hammer, Scimitar, Sickle

#### Push
**Effect**: When you hit a creature, you can push it **10 feet** straight away from you. If the creature is **Large or smaller**, it must succeed on a **STR save** against your Push DC (8 + ability modifier used for the attack + proficiency bonus) or be pushed. This push does not provoke opportunity attacks.

**Weapons**: Greatclub, Heavy Crossbow, Pike, Warhammer (versatile)

#### Sap
**Effect**: When you hit a creature, that creature has **disadvantage** on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

**Weapons**: Flail, Mace, Morningstar

#### Slow
**Effect**: When you hit a creature, its **speed is reduced by 10 feet** until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once with weapons that have this property, the speed reduction doesn't stack.

**Weapons**: Club, Javelin, Light Crossbow, Longbow, Whip

#### Topple
**Effect**: When you hit a creature, you can force it to make a **CON save** against your Topple DC (8 + ability modifier used for the attack + proficiency bonus). On a failure, the creature is knocked **Prone**.

**Weapons**: Battleaxe, Lance, Maul, Trident

#### Vex
**Effect**: When you hit a creature, you gain **advantage** on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

**Weapons**: Hand Crossbow, Longsword, Rapier, Shortsword, Handaxe

### Class Access to Weapon Mastery

| Class | Mastery Weapons at Level 1 | Additional |
|---|---|---|
| **Fighter** | 3 | +1 at levels 4, 10, 16 (max 6) |
| **Barbarian** | 2 | +1 at levels 4, 10 (max 4) |
| **Paladin** | 2 | +1 at levels 4, 10 (max 4) |
| **Ranger** | 2 | +1 at levels 4, 10 (max 4) |
| **Rogue** | 2 | +1 at level 9 (max 3) |
| **Monk** | 2 | Monk weapons only |

Full casters (Bard, Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard) do **not** gain Weapon Mastery as a class feature.

### Tactical Considerations

- **Graze** is excellent for Great Weapon Fighters: even on a miss, you deal your STR/DEX modifier as damage
- **Vex** is ideal for Rogues: hit with a rapier to gain advantage on your next attack, enabling Sneak Attack
- **Nick** with dual daggers or scimitars gives an extra attack without using your bonus action
- **Topple** followed by advantage from prone target is a strong combo for melee characters
- **Sap** on a tank's weapon reduces incoming damage to the party
- **Push** creates battlefield control without requiring spell slots
- **Slow** is excellent for ranged characters kiting melee enemies
- **Cleave** is situational but powerful against clustered enemies

title: 2024 Spellcasting Changes
summary: Multi-target spells roll damage once, ritual casting is streamlined, cantrips can be retrained, and prepared spell formulas are standardized.
content:
## 2024 Spellcasting Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Spellcasting receives several important rule changes that affect all caster classes.

### Multi-Target Damage: Roll Once

**2014**: Ambiguous — some tables rolled damage once for all targets, others rolled individually.

**2024**: When a spell deals damage to **multiple targets**, you make **one damage roll** and apply it to all targets. This is now an explicit rule, not table variation.

- Example: *Fireball* — roll 8d6 once. All targets who fail their save take the full result. Those who succeed take half.
- This speeds up play and is consistent with how many tables already played

### Ritual Casting (Streamlined)

- Any caster who has a spell with the **Ritual** tag **prepared** (or in their spellbook for Wizards) can cast it as a ritual
- Ritual casting adds **10 minutes** to the casting time (unchanged)
- Does not expend a spell slot (unchanged)
- The 2014 distinction between classes that could ritual cast and those that couldn't is simplified — if you can prepare it and it has the Ritual tag, you can ritual cast it

### Cantrip Retraining

- When you gain a level in a class, you can replace **one cantrip** you know from that class with another cantrip from that class's spell list
- This applies to all classes that learn cantrips
- 2014 had no cantrip swapping at all (you were locked into your choices permanently)

### Prepared Spells Formula

- Several classes that were **known casters** in 2014 are now **prepared casters** in 2024:
  - **Bard**: Now prepares spells (was known)
  - **Ranger**: Now prepares spells (was known)
  - **Sorcerer**: Now prepares spells (was known)
  - **Warlock**: Now prepares spells (was known)
- All prepared casters use the formula: **class level + spellcasting ability modifier**
- All can change their prepared spells after a **long rest**

### Combining Spell Effects

**2014**: The effects of the same spell cast multiple times don't combine; only the most potent applies.

**2024**: Clarified and expanded:
- The effects of **different spells** stack normally
- You cannot concentrate on **two spells** at once (unchanged)
- If two effects have the same name, only the most potent version applies (e.g., two *Bless* spells don't stack)

### Bonus Action Spells (Revised)

**2014 rule**: If you cast a spell as a bonus action, you can only cast a cantrip with your action.

**2024 rule**: This restriction is **loosened**:
- If you cast a spell with a bonus action, you can still cast a **cantrip or a spell of 1st level or higher** with your action, as long as that spell does not also require a bonus action
- Effectively, the restriction is now: you can't cast two bonus-action spells in the same turn
- This is a significant buff to classes with bonus action spells

### Spell Lists Reorganized

Spells are now organized by **class list** rather than by a universal spell list:
- Each class has its own curated spell list
- Some spells appear on multiple lists
- This replaces the 2014 approach where all spells were in one big list and each class picked from it (functionally the same but presented differently)

### Upcasting Clarification

- When a spell says "at higher levels," you can cast it using a higher-level spell slot for the enhanced effect
- Cantrips that scale do so based on your **total character level**, not class level (unchanged, but clarified for multiclass characters)

### Spell Component Changes

- Material components with a **gold cost** still require you to have the specific component
- Material components **without** a gold cost can be replaced by a spellcasting focus or component pouch (unchanged)
- Some specific spells have had their components revised

title: 2024 Feat System Changes
summary: Fighting styles are now feats, all characters get an origin feat at level 1, and Epic Boons replace the level 19 ASI.
content:
## 2024 Feat System Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Feats are restructured from an optional rule into a core part of character progression.

### Feats Are No Longer Optional

In 2014, feats were an **optional rule** that replaced an ASI. In 2024, feats are a **core rule** integrated into character creation and progression.

### Feat Categories

Feats are now organized into categories with level prerequisites:

#### Origin Feats (Level 1)
Granted by your background at character creation. Available origin feats:
- **Alert**: Can't be surprised, +5 to initiative, can swap initiative with a willing ally within 5 feet
- **Crafter**: Proficiency with 3 artisan's tools, craft items at 20% discount, create temporary items during short rests
- **Healer**: Use Healer's Kit as an action to restore 2d6 + 2 + target's level HP (once per creature per long rest)
- **Lucky**: 3 Luck Points per long rest; spend to gain advantage on a d20 Test or impose disadvantage on an attacker's roll
- **Magic Initiate**: 2 cantrips + 1 level 1 spell from a chosen class's list; can cast the level 1 spell once per long rest without a slot, or with slots if you have them
- **Musician**: Musical instrument proficiency; after a short or long rest, give up to PB allies Heroic Inspiration
- **Savage Attacker**: Once per turn, reroll melee weapon damage dice and take either result
- **Skilled**: 3 additional skill or tool proficiencies
- **Tavern Brawler**: Unarmed strikes deal 1d4 + STR. When you hit with an unarmed strike, you can push the target 5 feet. Enhanced improvised weapon damage.
- **Tough**: +2 HP per character level (retroactive and ongoing)

#### General Feats (Level 4+)
Taken at standard ASI levels (4, 8, 12, 16, 19). Examples include:
- **Ability Score Improvement**: +2 to one score or +1 to two scores (now a feat itself)
- **Actor**: Advantage on Deception to impersonate, +1 CHA
- **Athlete**: +1 STR or DEX, climb speed, standing from prone costs 5 ft
- **Charger**: When you Dash, you can make one melee attack with +1d8 damage or push 10 ft
- **Dual Wielder**: +1 AC when dual wielding, draw/stow two weapons at once, non-Light weapons can be dual-wielded if they're one-handed
- **Great Weapon Master**: On a hit with a Heavy weapon, deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus (replaces the -5/+10 mechanic)
- **Polearm Master**: Bonus action attack with polearm butt end (1d4), opportunity attack when creature enters your reach
- **Sentinel**: Creature you hit with opportunity attack has speed reduced to 0, can make opportunity attacks even if creature Disengages
- **Sharpshooter**: No disadvantage at long range, ignore half and three-quarters cover, deal extra damage equal to proficiency bonus with ranged weapons (replaces -5/+10)
- **War Caster**: Advantage on CON saves for concentration, can cast a spell as an opportunity attack

#### Fighting Style Feats (Level 1 for Fighters, Level 2 for Rangers/Paladins)
- All fighting styles from 2014 are now feats
- Martial classes get a bonus Fighting Style feat at the appropriate level
- Any character can take a Fighting Style feat at level 4+ if they meet prerequisites

#### Epic Boon Feats (Level 19)
- The level 19 ASI is replaced by an **Epic Boon** — a powerful capstone-tier feat
- Examples:
  - **Boon of Combat Prowess**: When you miss an attack, you can turn it into a hit (once per short/long rest)
  - **Boon of Dimensional Travel**: After any attack, you can teleport 30 feet (once per turn)
  - **Boon of Fate**: Add or subtract 2d4 from any d20 Test you can see (once per short/long rest)
  - **Boon of Irresistible Offense**: Choose a damage type; your attacks ignore resistance and treat immunity as resistance
  - **Boon of Recovery**: Once per long rest, when you drop to 0 HP, drop to your level in HP instead
  - **Boon of Speed**: Speed increases by 30 feet
  - **Boon of Spell Recall**: Cast a spell of 4th level or lower without a slot (once per long rest)

### Great Weapon Master and Sharpshooter Revised

The infamous **-5 to hit / +10 damage** trade from 2014 GWM and Sharpshooter is **removed**. Both feats now add your **proficiency bonus** to damage instead. This scales from +2 to +6 and doesn't require a penalty to attack rolls.
