title: 2024 Resting Rule Changes
summary: Long rests have a 16-hour cooldown, characters regain ALL hit dice on long rest, and you must have at least 1 HP to benefit from a long rest.
content:
## 2024 Resting Rule Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Resting rules receive important revisions that affect resource recovery pacing.

### Long Rest Changes

#### 16-Hour Cooldown

- You cannot benefit from a **long rest** unless at least **16 hours** have elapsed since your last long rest
- This prevents the 2014 exploit of taking back-to-back long rests
- A long rest is still at least **8 hours** (at least 6 hours sleeping, up to 2 hours of light activity)

#### Must Have 1 HP

- You must have **at least 1 hit point** at the start of the rest to benefit from a long rest
- This means a character at 0 HP who is stable but unconscious **cannot benefit** from a long rest until they regain at least 1 HP
- A stable creature regains 1 HP after 1d4 hours, so they can still eventually long rest — it just might take longer

#### Regain ALL Hit Dice

- **2014**: Regain half your total hit dice (rounded down, minimum 1) on a long rest
- **2024**: Regain **ALL** spent hit dice on a long rest
- This is a significant buff to healing between adventuring days
- Combined with the 16-hour cooldown, the design intent is: one long rest per day, but that rest is more restorative

#### Other Long Rest Benefits (Unchanged)

- Regain all hit points
- Regain all spell slots
- Regain all uses of features that recharge on a long rest
- Reduce exhaustion by 1 level (unchanged)

### Short Rest Changes

#### Duration

- A short rest is still at least **1 hour** of downtime (no change)
- You can spend hit dice during a short rest to regain HP (unchanged)
- For each hit die spent, roll the die + CON modifier to regain HP

#### No HP Minimum

- Unlike long rests, there is **no HP minimum** for a short rest
- A stable creature at 0 HP can benefit from a short rest if allies protect them

### Impact on Adventuring Day

The 2024 resting rules reinforce the design intent:
- **1 long rest per 24-hour period** (enforced by the 16-hour cooldown)
- **Multiple short rests** between long rests (no cooldown)
- Short-rest classes (Warlock, Fighter, Monk) benefit more from an adventuring day with multiple encounters
- Full-recovery long rests make single-encounter adventuring days more viable, but the cooldown prevents abuse

### Exhaustion and Resting

- Exhaustion is revised in 2024: it's now a **numerical scale** (Exhaustion 1, 2, 3, etc.) that imposes a penalty of **-2 per level** to all d20 Tests
- At **Exhaustion 10**, you die
- Each long rest reduces your Exhaustion level by **1**
- Some class features (Ranger's Tireless) can reduce Exhaustion on short rests

title: 2024 Condition Changes
summary: The Bloodied condition is reintroduced from 4e, Hide DC has a base of 15, and Exhaustion is revised to a numerical penalty system.
content:
## 2024 Condition Changes

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Conditions receive targeted revisions, with one major addition and one significant overhaul.

### Bloodied (Reintroduced)

- A creature is **Bloodied** when it has **half its hit point maximum or fewer**
- This is a condition, not just a description
- **Mechanical effect**: Primarily serves as a communication tool — players and DMs know when a creature is significantly wounded
- Some features and spells in the 2024 rules key off the Bloodied condition
- Originally from D&D 4th Edition, where it was a core mechanic with many triggered abilities

### Exhaustion (Overhauled)

**2014 Exhaustion**: 6 levels with escalating, different penalties at each level (disadvantage on checks, speed halved, disadvantage on attacks/saves, HP max halved, speed 0, death).

**2024 Exhaustion**: Simplified numerical system:
- Each level of Exhaustion imposes a **-2 penalty to all d20 Tests** (ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws)
- Penalties stack: Exhaustion 3 = -6 to all d20 Tests
- Your **speed is also reduced by 5 feet per level** of Exhaustion
- At **Exhaustion 10**, you **die**
- Each long rest reduces Exhaustion by **1 level**
- This is much simpler to track and more gradual in its impact

### Hide and Stealth (Revised)

**2014**: The DM sets the DC for Stealth checks, often opposed by Passive Perception.

**2024**:
- Taking the **Hide action** requires a **Stealth check against DC 15** (base DC)
- On success, you gain the **Invisible condition** (with limitations — you're not truly invisible, you're hidden)
- The creature remains hidden until:
  - It makes a sound louder than a whisper
  - An enemy finds it (using an action to make a Perception check against your Stealth result)
  - It attacks, casts a spell, or otherwise reveals itself
  - It is no longer behind cover or in heavy obscurement
- The **Invisible condition** from hiding works differently from the *Invisibility* spell:
  - Attacks against you have disadvantage
  - Your attacks have advantage
  - But if an enemy locates you (e.g., by hearing), they can target you even though you're "invisible"

### Incapacitated (Clarified)

- Cannot take **actions, bonus actions, or reactions** (unchanged)
- 2024 clarifies that Incapacitated also means you can't **concentrate on spells**
- Conditions that include Incapacitated (Stunned, Paralyzed, Unconscious, Petrified) inherit this

### Grappled (Revised)

**2014**: Speed becomes 0. You can use your action to make a contested Athletics/Acrobatics check to escape.

**2024**:
- Speed becomes **0** (unchanged)
- To escape, use your action to make a **STR or DEX save** (your choice) against the grappler's escape DC
- The grappler can **drag** the grappled creature at half speed (unchanged)
- The grapple ends if the grappler is Incapacitated or the grappled creature is moved out of reach by an external force

### Prone (Clarified)

- Melee attacks against a Prone creature have **advantage** within 5 feet (unchanged)
- Ranged attacks against a Prone creature have **disadvantage** (unchanged)
- Standing up costs **half your movement** (unchanged)
- Clarified: you **cannot stand up** if your speed is 0

### Other Condition Notes

- Most conditions are functionally unchanged from 2014
- The conditions list: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Exhaustion, Frightened, Grappled, Incapacitated, Invisible, Paralyzed, Petrified, Poisoned, Prone, Restrained, Stunned, Unconscious
- **Bloodied** is added to this list as the 16th condition

title: 2024 Notable Removals
summary: Artificer is absent from the 2024 PHB, Traits/Ideals/Bonds/Flaws removed, Half-Elf and Half-Orc removed as distinct species, Brutal Critical replaced, and more.
content:
## 2024 Notable Removals

*5e 2024 Rules Change*

Several features, options, and systems from the 2014 PHB and supplements are absent or replaced in the 2024 PHB.

### Artificer — Not in PHB

- The **Artificer** class, introduced in Eberron: Rising from the Last War and Tasha's Cauldron of Everything, is **not included** in the 2024 PHB
- It is not a core PHB class and may appear in a future supplement
- The 2014 Artificer from Tasha's remains usable with DM permission

### Half-Elf and Half-Orc — Removed as Distinct Species

- **Half-Elf** and **Half-Orc** are no longer separate species options
- Players who want a half-elf or half-orc character are encouraged to:
  - Choose **Elf** or **Human** (for half-elf) and flavor their appearance as mixed heritage
  - Choose **Orc** or **Human** (for half-orc) similarly
- This is part of the broader design philosophy of decoupling mechanics from ancestry

### Traits, Ideals, Bonds, and Flaws — Removed

- The structured **Personality Traits, Ideals, Bonds, and Flaws** tables are removed from backgrounds
- Players are still encouraged to develop their character's personality, but there are no mechanical tables to roll on
- This removes a step from character creation that many groups skipped anyway

### Brutal Critical — Replaced

- The Barbarian's **Brutal Critical** (extra damage dice on critical hits at levels 9, 13, 17) is removed
- Replaced by **Brutal Strike**, which offers tactical choices (push, slow, daze) in exchange for Reckless Attack advantage
- Brutal Strike is generally considered more interesting and reliable than extra crit damage

### Racial Ability Score Increases — Removed from Species

- Species no longer grant **+2/+1** or other ability score increases
- All ability score increases now come from **backgrounds**
- This was one of the most debated changes during the 2024 playtest

### Twinned Spell Metamagic — Removed

- The Sorcerer's **Twinned Spell** Metamagic option is not in the 2024 PHB
- This was one of the most powerful and frequently rules-lawyered Metamagic options
- Its removal simplifies Sorcerer play and eliminates ambiguous interactions

### Song of Rest — Removed from Bard

- The Bard's **Song of Rest** (extra healing during short rests) is removed
- This was a passive, often-forgotten feature

### Countercharm — Removed from Bard

- The Bard's **Countercharm** (advantage on saves vs charm/fright while performing) is removed
- Was widely considered one of the weakest class features in the game

### Favored Enemy (2014 version) — Removed from Ranger

- The original **Favored Enemy** (choose a creature type for tracking benefits) is replaced
- Now Favored Enemy grants Hunter's Mark as an always-prepared, eventually concentration-free spell

### Natural Explorer (2014 version) — Removed from Ranger

- The terrain-dependent **Natural Explorer** feature is removed
- Replaced by Deft Explorer (Expertise in a skill)

### Downtime Activities — Removed from PHB

- The 2024 PHB does not include **downtime activity rules** (crafting, running a business, etc.)
- These may appear in the 2024 DMG instead
- The 2014 PHB and Xanathar's downtime rules remain usable

### Alignment Restrictions

- No species or class has an **alignment restriction** or suggestion
- Alignment exists as a descriptor but carries no mechanical weight

### Multiclassing and Feats as Optional

- In 2014, both multiclassing and feats were **optional rules**
- In 2024, **feats are core** (integrated into backgrounds and progression)
- **Multiclassing** remains an optional rule with DM permission

### Subclasses Removed from PHB

Several subclasses present in the 2014 PHB are not in the 2024 PHB (4 subclasses per class in 2024 vs more in 2014):
- **Barbarian**: Path of the Berserker retained; Totem Warrior reimagined as Wild Heart
- **Cleric**: Only 4 domains (Life, Light, Trickery, War); Knowledge, Nature, Tempest removed from PHB
- **Wizard**: Only 4 schools (Abjuration, Divination, Evocation, Illusion); Conjuration, Enchantment, Necromancy, Transmutation removed from PHB
- These subclasses may appear in future supplements

title: 2024 Migration Guide
summary: Practical guide for DMs and players transitioning from 2014 to 2024 rules, covering backward compatibility, conflicts, and mixing editions.
content:
## 2024 Migration Guide

*Practical Transition Reference: 5e 2014 → 5e 2024*

This guide helps DMs and players navigate the transition between editions.

### Core Principle: Backward Compatibility

The 2024 PHB is designed to be **backward compatible** with 2014 content:
- Adventures written for 2014 work with 2024 characters without modification
- Monsters, magic items, and encounter design are unchanged
- The core resolution mechanic (d20 + modifiers vs DC/AC) is identical

### Approach 1: Full 2024 Adoption

**Best for**: New campaigns, groups ready to learn updated rules

1. All players create characters using 2024 rules
2. Use 2024 combat rules (weapon mastery, revised surprise, etc.)
3. Use 2024 feats (origin feats, epic boons)
4. Use 2024 spellcasting rules (prepared casters, multi-target damage)
5. Existing adventures run as-is

### Approach 2: Gradual Migration

**Best for**: Ongoing campaigns, groups who want to adopt changes incrementally

Suggested phase-in order:
1. **Phase 1 — Terminology and Conditions**: Adopt Species, d20 Test, Bloodied, revised Exhaustion
2. **Phase 2 — Combat Rules**: Adopt weapon mastery, revised surprise, unarmed strike saves, two-weapon fighting
3. **Phase 3 — Character Options**: Allow 2024 character creation for new characters; let existing characters rebuild using 2024 rules
4. **Phase 4 — Full Adoption**: Switch to 2024 class features, feats, and spellcasting

### Approach 3: Hybrid (Pick and Choose)

**Best for**: Groups who want specific improvements without a full switch

Highly recommended cherry-picks:
- **Weapon Mastery** — adds tactical depth to martial classes
- **Revised Two-Weapon Fighting** — frees up the bonus action
- **Origin Feats** — more interesting level 1 characters
- **Prepared Caster Bards/Rangers/Sorcerers** — quality of life improvement
- **Revised Exhaustion** — simpler to track
- **Long Rest Hit Dice Recovery** — regain all hit dice

### Known Conflicts and Edge Cases

#### 2014 Subclasses with 2024 Classes

- **Subclass level mismatch**: 2024 classes often gain subclass features at different levels than 2014. If using a 2014 subclass with a 2024 base class, you may need to adjust when subclass features are gained.
- **Rule**: Use the 2024 class's subclass feature levels. If the 2014 subclass has a feature at a level the 2024 class doesn't grant a subclass feature, the DM should assign it to the nearest 2024 subclass level.

#### Divine Smite

- If using 2024 Paladin, Divine Smite is a spell and cannot be used on crits reactively
- If using 2014 Paladin, the original class feature version applies
- **Do not mix**: Don't allow a character to use both the 2014 feature and 2024 spell versions

#### Feat Compatibility

- **Great Weapon Master and Sharpshooter**: The 2014 (-5/+10) and 2024 (+PB damage) versions are very different in power and feel. Choose one edition's version for your table.
- **Lucky**: The 2024 version works differently from the 2014 version. Pick one.
- Origin feats are new and have no conflict with 2014 rules.

#### Species vs Race

- If using 2014 species (races) with ASIs, either keep the ASIs or allow players to reassign them to match the 2024 background-based approach
- The Tasha's Cauldron "Custom Lineage" option is a good bridge

### Converting an Existing Character

Step-by-step for rebuilding a 2014 character as 2024:

1. **Species**: Keep your species. Remove its ASI; you'll get those from your background
2. **Background**: Choose a 2024 background (or custom). Assign +2/+1 or +1/+1/+1 to abilities. Gain an origin feat.
3. **Class**: Rebuild using 2024 class features. Note changed feature levels.
4. **Subclass**: If your subclass is in the 2024 PHB, use the 2024 version. If not, use the 2014 version with adjusted feature levels.
5. **Feats**: Replace any 2014 feats with their 2024 equivalents. Note that some feats are significantly changed (GWM, Sharpshooter, Lucky).
6. **Spells**: If your class changed from known to prepared, recalculate your prepared spells using the new formula.

### What to Tell Your Players

- The game is the same game — d20 + modifier vs DC
- Your character concept still works; the mechanics underneath are refined
- Martial characters are more interesting (weapon mastery)
- Caster characters are more flexible (prepared casting)
- Everyone gets a feat at level 1 now
- The biggest mechanical change: Paladin's Divine Smite is now a spell
